I want to explore and discover the boundary between what Tech@Lilly demands of Cloud and what Cloud must own internally.

**Shweta Choudhary** · *2026-03-12T17:44:41.477Z*

intros_outcomes.summary:
## Participants in the Room

- **Greg Johnson** — Leader, Private Cloud / Hosting / InfraOps (26-year Lilly veteran; brings hands-on AI/automation experience)
- **Chris Lesniewski** — AVP, Cloud & Connectivity
- **David Fleming** — Leader, AIOps
- **Mark Pearce** — Senior Director, Enterprise Connectivity
- **Todd Walters** — Enterprise Cloud Infrastructure Architect
- **Michelle Carr** — Infrastructure Business Manager
- **Karl Mayer** — Digital Core Lead Architect; Enterprise Business Architecture team member
- **Jonathan Klinginsmith** — Leader, Public Cloud & High Performance Computing
- **Greg Graf** — Automation Lead Architect
- **Shweta Choudhary** — Director, Cloud & Connectivity
- **Mary Jane Turnmire** — Leader, US Campus Strategy & Intern Program

---

## Desired Outcomes

- **Greg Johnson** — Leave with a defined strategy, direction, and 3-year vision that can guide his teams with clarity
- **David Fleming** — Organize thinking around agentic use cases and begin building a concrete backlog
- **Mark Pearce** — Surface ideas from across teams, identify overlapping opportunities, and establish a foundation for cross-team collaboration
- **Mary Jane Turnmire** — Understand how agentic tools can be applied to campus and intern program management workflows
- **Jonathan Klinginsmith** — Have an open, substantive discussion about high-impact automation and AI opportunities aligned to Agentic Enterprise goals
- **Chris Lesniewski** — Produce a categorized list of agentic ideas with enough context to determine a credible path forward for each
- **Todd Walters** — Establish a shared baseline understanding of where the group stands collectively on embracing and enabling the Agentic Enterprise
- **Karl Mayer** — Clarify the boundary between what Tech@Lilly requires of Cloud and what Cloud must own and govern internally
- **Michelle Carr** — Identify process improvement opportunities specifically within the team's financial operations
- **Shweta Choudhary** — Ideate on the full scope of Agentic Enterprise possibilities for Cloud & Connectivity and translate that into a roadmap

---

## Common Themes

- **Agentic Enterprise as the Central Organizing Idea** — The majority of participants (Fleming, Klinginsmith, Lesniewski, Walters, Choudhary, Graf) explicitly framed their desired outcomes around agentic AI — whether building a backlog, scoping opportunities, or defining an enablement path. This is the dominant lens through which the group is approaching the session.

- **From Ideas to Action — Prioritization and Roadmapping** — Multiple participants are not satisfied with ideation alone. Lesniewski wants categorization and an approach; Choudhary wants a roadmap; Fleming wants a backlog; Johnson wants a 3-year strategy. There is a strong, shared pull toward structured, decision-ready outputs rather than open-ended brainstorming.

- **Cross-Team Alignment and Boundary Clarity** — Pearce, Walters, and Mayer each surfaced a variation of the same need: understanding where teams are relative to one another, where collaboration is possible, and where ownership and accountability lines sit. This reflects an underlying tension between team-level autonomy and enterprise-level coherence.

---

## Shared Definition of Success

The group will consider today a success if they leave with a shared, prioritized view of agentic AI opportunities across Cloud & Connectivity — organized well enough to inform a 3-year strategy and near-term roadmap. Equally important, participants want clarity on how their individual team efforts connect, overlap, and align so that the path forward is coordinated rather than fragmented.
